description_long: This is a tall, freestanding clock tower constructed primarily from dark, polished wood, featuring a rectangular base and a prominent, ornate upper section. The front of the tower displays a circular clock face with black Arabic numerals and two black hands, set within a white background, framed by a decorative wooden arch supported by slender columns. Below the clock face, a glass-paneled section reveals a set of brass pendulums and weights, adding both visual interest and functional detail. The sides of the tower are solid wood with subtle paneling, while the back is plain and unadorned. The roof is a gently arched, gabled structure, lending a classical and elegant silhouette to the tower. The overall design is reminiscent of traditional Neoclassical or Victorian-era clock towers, suitable for placement in public squares, parks, or building entrances.
description: A tall, dark wooden clock tower with a white circular clock face, glass-paneled pendulum section, and an arched gabled roof. The design is ornate and classical, with visible brass pendulums and decorative woodwork.
description_view_0: Front view showing the white clock face with black numerals, glass panel revealing brass pendulums, and decorative arch supported by columns.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the tower's narrow profile, solid wood construction, and layered paneling with no visible clock face or glass.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain, unadorned wooden back with the arched roof visible but no clock face or glass panel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the narrow wooden profile and absence of decorative or functional features.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular with a white background, black Arabic numerals marking each hour, and two black hands for hours and minutes. The face is simple and functional, with no additional decorative elements or markings beyond the numerals and hands.
